21.correct: The President of India appoints the Prime Minister - conventionally the leader of the party or coalition commanding a majority in the Lok Sabha.

wrong.A: Parliament does not appoint the PM; it is the body in which the PM must command confidence.
wrong.B: The Chief Justice administers oaths but does not appoint the PM.
wrong.D: The Vice-President has no role in appointing the PM.

21.fact: The PM and the Council of Ministers are appointed by the President but are responsible to the Lok Sabha.

22.correct: Article 368 empowers Parliament to amend the Constitution and lays down the procedure for doing so.

wrong.B: Article 356 deals with President's Rule in a state.
wrong.D: Article 370 dealt with the special status of Jammu & Kashmir.

22.fact: The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) held that Parliament cannot amend the 'basic structure' of the Constitution.

23.correct: The poet Vallathol Narayana Menon founded the Kerala Kalamandalam to preserve and promote Kathakali and other Kerala art forms.

wrong.A: 'Natya Shastra Foundation' is not the institution he founded.
wrong.B: 'Kerala Nrityam Academy' is not the correct name.

23.fact: Kerala Kalamandalam, established in 1930, is now a deemed university for the performing arts.

24.correct: Giddha is a folk dance of women (mainly in Punjab) featuring rhythmic clapping and the singing of short verses called 'bolian'.

wrong.A: It is not a male-dominated martial-art form.
wrong.C: It is a folk dance, not a classical dance style.
wrong.D: It is a group dance by women, not a male solo.

24.fact: Giddha is the female counterpart of the energetic male Bhangra dance.

25.correct: 'The God of Small Things' was written by Arundhati Roy, who won the Booker Prize for it in 1997.

wrong.C: Shekhar Kapur is a film director, not the author.
wrong.D: Kiran Desai wrote 'The Inheritance of Loss'.

25.fact: It was Arundhati Roy's debut novel and remains her best-known work of fiction.

26.correct: The Aravalli Range is considered the oldest mountain range in India (and among the oldest in the world), formed by ancient fold movements.

wrong.A: The Himalayas are among the youngest fold mountains.
wrong.C: The Western Ghats are block mountains of the peninsular edge, younger than the Aravallis.
wrong.D: The Eastern Ghats are also younger than the Aravallis.

26.fact: Guru Shikhar on Mount Abu is the highest peak of the Aravalli Range.

27.correct: The Brahmaputra river forms Majuli, the largest river island in the world, located in Assam.

wrong.A: The Ganga does not form Majuli.
wrong.C: The Godavari is a peninsular river.
wrong.D: The Yamuna is a tributary of the Ganga.

27.fact: Majuli is a major centre of Assam's Neo-Vaishnavite Satra monasteries.

28.correct: A rainbow is an optical effect formed at a fixed angle relative to the observer; as the student moves towards it, it appears to stay at the same distance and can never be reached.

wrong.A: One cannot actually reach a rainbow.
wrong.B: The rainbow does not vanish simply because one moves towards it.
wrong.D: The colours do not change as one approaches.

28.fact: A rainbow forms because raindrops refract, internally reflect and disperse sunlight at about a 42-degree angle.

29.correct: The stained-glass windows in the west of St Paul's tradition were designed by Sir Edward Burne-Jones, a leading Pre-Raphaelite artist known for his stained-glass work.

wrong.A: Leonardo da Vinci was a Renaissance master, not the designer of these windows.
wrong.C: Michelangelo is famous for sculpture and the Sistine Chapel, not these windows.
wrong.D: Rembrandt was a Dutch painter, unrelated to this stained glass.

29.fact: Burne-Jones collaborated with William Morris's firm on many famous stained-glass commissions.

30.correct: A lunar eclipse occurs when the Earth comes between the Sun and the Moon, blocking sunlight from reaching the Moon and casting Earth's shadow on it.

wrong.A: A solar eclipse occurs when the Moon blocks sunlight from reaching the Earth.
wrong.C: A meteor shower is caused by debris burning in the atmosphere, unrelated to shadows.
wrong.D: The aurora (northern lights) is caused by solar particles in the atmosphere.

30.fact: A lunar eclipse can occur only on a full-moon night when Sun, Earth and Moon are aligned.

31.correct: The 1992 Cricket World Cup (held in Australia and New Zealand) was sponsored by and known as the Benson & Hedges World Cup.

wrong.A: The 1987 World Cup was the Reliance Cup (India/Pakistan).
wrong.C: The 1996 World Cup was the Wills World Cup (subcontinent).
wrong.D: The 1999 World Cup was held in England.

31.fact: The 1992 World Cup was the first to use coloured clothing, white balls and day-night matches; Pakistan won it.

32.correct: Weightlifter Mirabai Chanu won India's first gold medal at the 2022 Commonwealth Games (Birmingham).

wrong.B: Bajrang Punia won a wrestling gold but not India's first at the 2022 CWG.
wrong.C: Jeremy Lalrinnunga won a weightlifting gold, but after Mirabai Chanu.
wrong.D: P.V. Sindhu won a badminton gold later in the Games.

32.fact: Mirabai Chanu had earlier won a silver medal at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics.

33.correct: Cardiac (heart) muscles are classified as involuntary muscles because they contract automatically without conscious control.

wrong.A: They are not under voluntary control.
wrong.C: Skeletal muscles are voluntary and striated; cardiac muscle, though striated, is involuntary.
wrong.D: Smooth muscle is involuntary too, but heart muscle is specifically cardiac muscle.

33.fact: Cardiac muscle is unique: it is striated like skeletal muscle yet involuntary like smooth muscle, and never tires.

34.correct: The pituitary gland secretes growth hormone (somatotropin), which controls body growth and development.

wrong.A: The thyroid gland secretes thyroxine, which regulates metabolism.
wrong.B: The adrenal gland secretes adrenaline and cortisol.
wrong.D: The pancreas secretes insulin and digestive enzymes.

34.fact: The pituitary is called the 'master gland' because it controls several other endocrine glands.

35.correct: Kanyakumari (Cape Comorin) is the southernmost point of the Indian mainland, where three water bodies meet.

wrong.A: Indira Point (Great Nicobar) is the southernmost point of Indian territory, but not of the mainland.
wrong.B: Rameshwaram is a pilgrimage island, not the southern tip.
wrong.D: Kovalam is a beach in Kerala, not the southernmost point.

35.fact: At Kanyakumari the Arabian Sea, the Bay of Bengal and the Indian Ocean meet.

36.correct: Under the Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY), maternity benefits had been provided to over about 4.05 crore beneficiaries by July 2025.
36.fact: PMMVY provides cash incentives to pregnant and lactating mothers for the first living child.

37.correct: Tipu Sultan was called the 'Sher-e-Mysore' (Tiger of Mysore) for his fierce resistance against the British.

wrong.B: Haidar Ali was Tipu's father and predecessor, not the one called Tiger of Mysore.
wrong.C: Alivardi Khan was the Nawab of Bengal.
wrong.D: Mahadji Shinde was a Maratha leader.

37.fact: Tipu Sultan died in 1799 defending his capital Srirangapatna in the Fourth Anglo-Mysore War.

38.correct: The historic Lucknow Pact between the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League was signed in 1916, promoting Hindu-Muslim unity for self-government.

wrong.A: 1929 is associated with the Nehru Report era, not the Lucknow Pact.
wrong.C: 1919 was the year of the Rowlatt Act and Jallianwala Bagh, not the Pact.

38.fact: The Lucknow Pact was facilitated by leaders including Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

39.correct: A.R. Rahman won two Academy Awards in 2009 for 'Slumdog Millionaire' - for Best Original Score and Best Original Song ('Jai Ho').

wrong.A: Pritam did not compose the Slumdog Millionaire score.
wrong.B: Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy did not win these Oscars.
wrong.D: Vishal-Shekhar were not the composers of this film.

39.fact: Rahman also won two Grammy Awards for the same film's music.

40.correct: In 2025 the IMF projected India's GDP growth for 2025 at about 6.4%, keeping India among the fastest-growing major economies.

wrong.A: 4.4% is far below the projected rate.
wrong.C: 7.1% is higher than the IMF's projection.

40.fact: The IMF publishes its growth forecasts in the World Economic Outlook (WEO) reports.
